samples.xml_description:
Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers. Clock signal. Reset signal. Signed or unsigned operation. Input operand DIVIDEND. Input operand DIVISOR. Valid operation request. The output has been acknowledged. The result is valid and ready. Output result. 0 1 2 3 4 5 4'b1000 1'b1 The negated absolute value of the divisor. The shift register. The subtraction result. The multiplexer result. The carry out of the subtraction.

When

opn_valid is high and res_valid is low,

the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.

Initializes the shift register sr with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.

Sets neg_divisor to the negated absolute value of the divisor.

Sets the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.

During the division process (If start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps):

  1. If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete:

    • cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
    • Updates the shift register sr with the final remainder and quotient.

  2. Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register sr is updated based on the subtraction result:

    • Computes the subtraction of neg_divisor.
    • Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.
    • Updates sr by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.

After the division process is complete, res_valid is set to 1 to indicate that the result is ready.

When res_valid is high and res_ready is low, the module asserts res_ready to indicate that the result is available.

When res_valid and res_ready are both high, res_valid is de-asserted to indicate that the result has been consumed.
